---
title: "First draft"
output: html_document
---

The model structure

First we have

$\mu_j$ the mean abilities of a class to do skill $j\in \{+,-,\times,\div, \int,\frac{d}{dx}\}$ and $\sigma_i$ the standard deviation of the class at each of these abilities.

$$
\mu_{1,j} \sim N(0,1) 
$$

$$
\sigma_j \sim Exp(1)
$$

then the abilities of each individual student the $k$'th student will be $\alpha_{i,j,k}$

$$
\alpha_{i,j,k}|\mu_{1,j},\sigma_j,\alpha_{i,j,k-1} \sim N(\mu_{1,j} +\alpha_{i-1,j,k},\sigma_i^2)
$$

this update happens every test

we want the value of the individuals abilities to also vary relative to the group over time...

so when we initialize we have the initial distribution of abilities mean of the class and then i vary the individuals abilities can fluctuate over time

now we have a the steps in the problem

$S_{i,j,k,l}|S_{i,j^*,k,l-1},\alpha_{ijk} \sim Bern(S_l\cdot(logistic(\alpha_{ijk}-\frac{1}{t}))$

So to simplify this we will only consider 1 test.

Thus we will have a bunch of students and only one test.
